summary: American film director (1928–1999), one of cinema's most technically innovative auteurs. Known for meticulous cinematography, use of existing music, and a wide range of genres. Major works include 2001: A Space Odyssey, A Clockwork Orange, Barry Lyndon, The Shining, Dr. Strangelove, and Eyes Wide Shut. Started as a photographer for Look magazine.

works.name: 2001: A Space Odyssey (1968)
works.description: Kubrick's landmark science fiction film. The opening popularized Richard Strauss's 'Thus Spoke Zarathustra.' 'The Blue Danube' plays during a space station docking sequence. Kubrick originally hired Alex North to write a score but rejected the entire score without telling North and used existing music by Gyorgy Ligeti and Richard Strauss instead. President-elect JFK saw Kubrick's earlier film Spartacus over protests from the American Legion for employing Dalton Trumbo as screenwriter. Wikipedia

clues.clue: The opening popularized Richard Strauss's 'Thus Spoke Zarathustra'; 'The Blue Danube' plays during a space station docking sequence
examples: The Blue Danube plays during a space station docking sequence in one of this director's films, whose opening popularized Richard Strauss's Thus Spoke Zarathustra.

clues.clue: Kubrick hired Alex North to compose the score but rejected it entirely without informing North, using existing pieces by Gyorgy Ligeti and Richard Strauss instead
examples: In 1968, this director hired Alex North to write a last-minute score but, without telling North, rejected the entire score and instead used existing pieces by Gyorgy Ligeti and Richard Strauss.

works.name: A Clockwork Orange (1971)
works.description: Alex (the protagonist) and his gang visit 'Home,' where they beat up writer F. Alexander and rape his wife to the tune of 'Singin' in the Rain.' The Ludovico process is used to cure Alex of 'ultraviolence,' inadvertently making him hate Beethoven's music. The main theme is an arrangement of Henry Purcell's Music for the Funeral of Queen Mary for the Moog synthesizer by Wendy Carlos. William Tell Overture accompanies a three-minute timelapse of a threesome. The bar 'Korova' is plastered with the word 'moloko' (Russian for milk); furniture inspired by Allen Jones's artworks (Hatstand, Table, Chair). Set features sculptures by Herman Makkink (Rocking Machine; four Christs dancing arm-in-arm) and a painting by Kubrick's wife. Milena Canonero's debut costume designs were for this and another Kubrick film. Wikipedia

works.name: Barry Lyndon (1975)
works.description: Period drama about Redmond Barry/Barry Lyndon, who climbs the English social ladder but loses his left leg in a duel. Cinematography inspired by a scrapbook of classic English paintings (especially Hogarth); slow outward zooms make frames resemble 18th-century paintings. Shot entirely by candlelight using special NASA Zeiss lenses (f-stop 0.7). Actors moved laterally to avoid going out of focus. Actors (including the protagonist meeting a countess at a card table) are lit using super-fast Zeiss lenses inspired by Hogarth paintings. Features the flute and snare drum playing 'The British Grenadiers' during battle scenes. The last shot recreates the final piece in Hogarth's Marriage-a-la-Mode series. Grew out of Kubrick's scrapped Napoleon biopic idea. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Shot entirely by candlelight using special NASA Zeiss lenses (f-stop 0.7); actors moved laterally to stay in focus

works.name: The Shining (1980)
works.description: Ends with Kubrick personally airbrushing Jack Nicholson into a 1921 New Year's Eve party photograph, played to 'Midnight, the Stars, and You.' The two Grady twin girls were inspired by Diane Arbus's photograph Identical Twins. Danny riding around the Overlook Hotel on his tricycle was filmed using the newly invented Steadicam (invented by Garrett Brown). Wikipedia

works.name: Eyes Wide Shut (1999)
works.description: Kubrick's final film, adapted from Arthur Schnitzler's Austrian novella Dream Story. Tom Cruise's character finds his way into a secretive masked orgy held at a former Rothschild home. The protagonist enters by saying the word 'fidelio.' The orgy's score: first half uses reversed Romanian liturgy vocals by Jocelyn Pook; second half features Sri Lankan musician Manickam Yogeswaran reciting a verse from the Gita (later removed due to protests by South African Hindus). A blindfolded pianist (played by director Todd Field) plays in a room. Cate Blanchett voiced uncredited lines for a woman in the orgy sequence. Kubrick met his third wife Christiane at a masked ball at which he was the only participant without a costume. Almost every scene only has natural light from a Christmas tree. Wikipedia

works.name: Dr. Strangelove or: How I Learned to Stop Worrying and Love the Bomb (1964)
works.description: A dark comedy about nuclear war. General Jack D. Ripper orders a nuclear attack on the Soviet Union. Major Kong waves his hat while riding an atomic bomb after 'Wing Attack Plan R' is issued. Ends with Vera Lynn's 'We'll Meet Again' playing over nuclear explosions as a man worries about a 'mineshaft gap.' Inspired in part by Herman Kahn, a RAND Corporation analyst who wrote On Thermonuclear War. The 'I'm Spartacus' line from Kubrick's earlier Spartacus was parodied in the film adaptation of Lolita by Clare Quilty. Wikipedia

works.name: Paths of Glory (1957)
works.description: A WWI film depicting the brutality of trench warfare. Ends with a German prisoner singing 'The Faithful Hussar' to a group of soldiers. After starring in this film, Kirk Douglas again worked with Kubrick on Spartacus. Kubrick met his wife Christiane on the set of this film. Wikipedia

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: Stanley Kubrick (1928–1999) started his career as a teenager when he sold a staged photo of a newsstand employee looking sad over FDR's death to Look magazine. He then made his first film, the documentary Day of the Fight, adapted from a photoessay on boxer Walter Cartier. The Seafarers International Union commissioned his first color film, which drew on Eisenstein's Strike. His debut feature was The Killing (based on Lionel White's novel Clean). His family lived at Childwickbury Manor in Hertfordshire. He popularized the tracking shot across films. Wikipedia

comprehensive_summary:
Stanley Kubrick (1928–1999) began his career in photography, selling a staged photo of a newsstand employee reacting to FDR's death to Look magazine as a teenager. His first film was the documentary Day of the Fight, based on a photoessay on boxer Walter Cartier; the Seafarers International Union commissioned his first color film, which drew on motifs from Eisenstein's Strike. His debut narrative feature was The Killing (based on Lionel White's novel Clean), which involves sharpshooter Nikki Arcane taking out a horse to distract a crowd during a heist.

2001: A Space Odyssey (1968) opened with Richard Strauss's 'Thus Spoke Zarathustra' and features 'The Blue Danube' during a space station docking sequence. Kubrick commissioned Alex North to write a score but rejected it entirely without informing North, using pre-existing pieces by Gyorgy Ligeti and Richard Strauss instead.

A Clockwork Orange (1971) features protagonist Alex and his gang beating up writer F. Alexander and raping his wife to 'Singin' in the Rain'; Alex is then subjected to the Ludovico conditioning process to cure him of 'ultraviolence,' inadvertently making him unable to tolerate Beethoven. The Korova bar is plastered with the Russian word for milk ('moloko'); its furniture was inspired by Allen Jones's artworks (Hatstand, Table, Chair). The set features Herman Makkink sculptures (Rocking Machine; four Christs dancing arm-in-arm) and a painting by Kubrick's wife. The main theme is Purcell's Music for the Funeral of Queen Mary arranged for Moog synthesizer by Wendy Carlos; the William Tell Overture accompanies a three-minute timelapse of a threesome.

Barry Lyndon (1975) is a period drama in which the title character climbs the English social ladder but loses his left leg in a duel. Kubrick used NASA Zeiss lenses (f-stop 0.7) to shoot entirely by candlelight, inspired by Hogarth paintings; the camera slowly zooms outward throughout to mimic the framing of 18th-century paintings. The final shot recreates the last piece in Hogarth's Marriage-a-la-Mode series. The film grew out of Kubrick's scrapped Napoleon biopic idea.

The Shining (1980) ends with Kubrick personally airbrushing Jack Nicholson into a 1921 New Year's Eve party photograph, played to 'Midnight, the Stars, and You.' The Grady twins were inspired by Diane Arbus's photograph Identical Twins. Danny's tricycle scenes were filmed using the newly invented Steadicam by Garrett Brown.

Dr. Strangelove (1964) depicts General Jack D. Ripper ordering a nuclear attack on the Soviet Union; Major Kong waves his hat while riding an atomic bomb. The film ends with Vera Lynn's 'We'll Meet Again' as a character frets about a 'mineshaft gap.' It was inspired in part by RAND Corporation analyst Herman Kahn's On Thermonuclear War.

Eyes Wide Shut (1999), Kubrick's final film, was adapted from Arthur Schnitzler's Dream Story. Tom Cruise infiltrates a masked orgy at a former Rothschild home by saying the password 'fidelio.' The orgy's score features Jocelyn Pook's reversed Romanian liturgy and originally included Sri Lankan Manickam Yogeswaran reciting the Bhagavad Gita (removed after protests). Cate Blanchett voiced a character's lines uncredited. A blindfolded pianist was played by director Todd Field.

Paths of Glory (1957) depicts WWI trench warfare and ends with a German prisoner singing 'The Faithful Hussar.' Kirk Douglas starred before reuniting with Kubrick on Spartacus (1960); Kubrick met his wife Christiane on the Paths of Glory set. Spartacus inspired the famous 'I'm Spartacus' solidarity line, later parodied in the Lolita adaptation and Monty Python's Life of Brian.

recursive_suggestions: 2001: A Space Odyssey — likely has its own rich clue set; HAL 9000 and the monolith
A Clockwork Orange — rich with visual and musical clues; worth standalone search
Barry Lyndon — cinematography and Hogarth connections are detailed and recurring
The Shining — likely has standalone clue set (Overlook Hotel, REDRUM, etc.)
Dr. Strangelove — black comedy with specific memorable scenes
Eyes Wide Shut — masked orgy sequence has multiple layers of clues
